title: Fondamenta de Fazza l'Arsenal
description: Arsenal ist der Name der Schiffswerft, des Zeughauses und der Flottenbasis der ehemaligen Republik Venedig. Der Bau des Arsenals wurde unter dem Dogen Ordelaf Falier ab 1104 begonnen. Das Grundstück bestand aus zwei sumpfigen Inseln im Stadtteil Castello. Diese Werft, die als größter Produktionsbetrieb Europas vor dem Zeitalter der Industrialisierung betrachtet werden kann, wurde zum Vorbild für andere Marinearsenale in Europa.

title: Anonymous Stateless Immigrants Pavilion
tags: venice italy brick wall stencil political plaster statement immigrants anonymous venezia humanitarian veneto stateless
description:
I assumed that this was a political (or humanitarian) statement and that there was no such pavilion. If there was, I did not see it.

There was a pavilion devoted to near-extinct languages, kept alive by ever shrinking and ever aging populations. It included audio of the languages being spoken.
comments: fabulous circle: This was from the Venetian biennale. The park is only open during the actual event, and it's full of national pavilions. www.labiennale.org/en/biennale/venues/pavilions.html?back... These were stenciled all over the city during summer 2014 biennale essentially pointing all over the place, presumably as a commentary regarding all the immigrant street vendors around. | UrbanphotoZ: [https://www.flickr.com/photos/lapajaritafrenetica] One of the fascinating things about Venice (to me, anyway) was that graffiti that I saw 2 and 4 years ago was still there when I was back this year. I had not seen, or at least, had not noticed these stencils when I was there last (although it was before the start of the Biennale). I was sort of hoping that they were a commentary on the treatment of the refugees who are flooding Italy and other European countries from the Middle East and Africa, too often dying in the process. Well, it's still my hope. | fabulous circle: I am sure it is! It's not like its new.... It's funny to me how places like Italy seem to still be surprised about immigration when this process of high immigration has now been happening for a couple of decades. | caring tax: We in the U.S. are sort of isolated from the happenings in the crowded work for some reason. Seems we are the most open bordered nation that there is. But, somehow we seem very isolated and narrow in our consciousness about the everyday lives of other people in the world. We need one of these. You would probably find a guy named Snowden living here. | bigsassysmurf: they have no pavilion : )

title: Marathon of the Unexpected
description: Left to right, top to bottom (da sinistra a destra, dall'alto in basso): "I ki ni kiku" by Noriko Sasaki & Rieko Okada, installation by Yukikoh Okura / "Nylon 3 - work in progress" by Polyxeni Angelidou / "Arigato" by Marianna Batelli / "La gabbia" by Lorenzo Meid Pagani. Performance: 24 giugno 2012, Teatro Piccolo Arsenale

title: Marathon of the Unexpected
description: Left to right, top to bottom (da sinistra a destra, dall'alto in basso): "Incarnazione" by Laura Boato / "Strategies of the Imperceptible" by Nathalie Larquet / "Crazy Light Moon (Studio 2)" by Pietro Pireddu / "Mi cuerpo es mi casa (Corpo Terra)" by Pilar Gallegos. Performance: 24 giugno 2012, Teatro Piccolo Arsenale

title: Marathon of the Unexpected
description: Left to right, top to bottom (da sinistra a destra, dall'alto in basso): "Überich, Saudade" by Oren Lazovski / "Assassins For One Night" by Alexey Taran / "Variazioni (Frame e Variazione I)" by Margherita Pirotto / "The Two Queens Souls" by Beatrice Paoleschi. Performance: 24 giugno 2012, Teatro Piccolo Arsenale

title: Marathon of the Unexpected
description: Left to right, top to bottom (da sinistra a destra, dall'alto in basso): "Kawaii" by Schuko (Marta Melucci & Francesca Telli) / "Rewind Studio #1" by Colaps / "Vanitas" by Barbara Stimoli / "Distrazione di massa - Oggetto coreografico #1" by Raffaele Irace (with/con Vittoria Carpegna & Vanessa Michielon). Performance: 24 giugno 2012, Teatro Piccolo Arsenale

title: Marathon of the Unexpected
description: Left to right, top to bottom (da sinistra a destra, dall'alto in basso): "Il trespolo di Claudio Mirel" by Francesco Schiavulli / "Last Time Next Door" by Doris Heusser / "Replay" by 7-8 chili / "Mani piene di vento" by Cie Tango Ostinato. Performance: 24 giugno 2012, Teatro Piccolo Arsenale

title: Fork Shadow Quartet
tags: venice italy london italian europe italia photographer shadows fork venezia cutlery apps quartet iphone diptic viveca ipics phoneography iphoneography itogs ipadography instagram
description: via Instagram instagr.am/p/KxhK4CDN00/ © Viveca Koh - Please do not use my images without permission. Website | Blog | Twitter | G+ | Facebook
comments: Blunders500 [ www.markblundellphoto.com ]: Thats nice . . . | photocillin: Four Forks sake.......... nice tetriptych | vetbonkie: Amazing shadows! | Viveca Koh FRPS: It was early evening and the long shadows were fantastic, every time I moved the fork the shadow changed - who would have thought that cutlery could be so photogenic? | vetbonkie: So often it is so in the details, i realy like this! | *•●☆★☆ bumble~bee ☆★☆●•*: Interesting. I like it | Alex E. Proimos: Love the contrast. So Vivid! Enjoy the week. Please enjoy my latest portrait: Eye of Emotion (Lima, Peru)
